title: Lerchea beccariana
taxonomy: Lerchea beccariana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Lerchea and is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is further categorized into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the rank of class, it falls under Magnoliopsida, and the order is Gentianales. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place in the botanical hierarchy.

intro: Lerchea beccariana, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, is a type of shrub or vine belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Malaysia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Lerchea beccariana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicine formulas for the treatment of various ailments.
